aru: lani ne na ra ya Aru.
na ra mene.
ta ra mene nela.
u ra melo ne na.
na mun ra kale mini.
na ra waro.
ta ra lora ke na?
na ra lora ke ta.
na ra waro we ya Mira.
ta o waro mini.
na ra sela ke luma.
u ra sela ke malo maru.
ta ra lora ke noki?
en: Do you hear the night / darkness?

aru: mata ne na ra sela ke tulu.
nuko ne ta ra lora ke waro.
na ra naka.
ta ra naka ko malo.
u ra tanu so malo.
na ra weki so malo.
na ra naka we ta.
na ra to malo.
na ra to insa malo.
tulu ra to supa malo.
en: The tool is on top of the house.

aru: kano ra to poka mamu.
en: The child is beside the parent.

aru: malo ra to kima wai.
temu luma nu le, na ra salo.
temu luma pa le, na ra pa miso.
temu luma po le, na ra po tanu.
na ra nu kani ke pani.
ta ra po naka ko tera para.
en: You will go to a far place.

aru: na ra kani ke pani.
ta o kani ke pani welo.
na ra rima ke wai.
pani ra to suko.
en: The food is in the container.

aru: wai ra nilo.
na ra maku ke malo.
ta ra salo we tulu.
u ra raku ke sipu.
na ra lowa ke lipa.
tulu i ra ko sela para.
en: This tool is for distant seeing.

aru: na ra nema ke ya Aru.
ta ra sona ke naru ne waro.
en: You know the rules of speech.

aru: u ra sona maku ke tulu.
en: They know how to make a tool.

aru: na ra rima sona waro nela.
en: I want to know how to speak well.

aru: senu ra pane ke se?
ka ta ra kani?
se ra naka?
ta ra sela ke se?
ta ra to se?
ta ra no tanu so etu se?
na ra no kani ke pani.
no na ra kani ke pani.
na ra kani ke no pani.
o no naka.
na ra pa no tanu.
na ra no naka so etu nilo.
en: I do not go because of cold.

aru: na ra no naka so etu i le, u ra nilo.
en: I do not go because it is cold.

aru: u ra miso so etu salo maru.
en: They rest because of much work.

aru: ta ra tanu so etu rima ne na?
en: Did you come because of my wish?

aru: u ra naka, sia na ra naka.
malo ne na ra maru.
lani ne ta ra ya Mira.
naru ne waro ra lisu.
malo ko kano ra kima.
en: The house for the child is near.

aru: wai so rano ra lisu.
en: The water from the river is clean.

aru: mene re ri ra maku ke malo, ra nela.
en: The person who builds a house is good.

aru: malo re na ra sela ke ri, ra maru.
en: The house that I see is big.

aru: mene re na ra lano ke pani ko ri, ra melo ne na.
en: The person to whom I give food is my friend.

aru: tulu re ta ra kipa ke ri, ra kasa.
en: The tool you hold is hard.

aru: malo re na ra to ri, ra nilo.
en: The house where I am is cold.

aru: malo i ra maru pasu malo ena.
en: This house is bigger than that house.

aru: tulu i ra nela pasu tulu ena.
en: This tool is better than that tool.

aru: wai i ra welo mini pasu wai ena.
en: This water is less hot than that water.

aru: mene i ra riki sama pasu mene ena.
en: This person is as fast as that one.

aru: malo i ra maru pasu ora.
na ra sona ke i le, u ra pa tanu.
ta ra rima ke i le, na ra naka.
u ra sela ke i le, na ra maku ke malo.
en: They see that I build a house.

aru: na ra waro ke i le, ya Aru ra lisu.
en: I say that Aru is simple.

aru: ta ra sano ke i le, na ra po tanu?
en: Do you believe that I will come?

aru: soma ne na ra nilo.
na ra nawa to pamo.
en: I have pain in my leg.

aru: kute ne ta ra kipa ke tulu.
muto ne u ra no waro.
neno ne na ra pawa.
kasi ra to tera.
en: The plant is on the ground.

aru: simo ra naka to rano.
en: The animal moves by the river.

aru: roka ra tanu so kawo.
kowa ra naka we luma.
en: The wind moves with the light / sun.

aru: mewa ra maru pasu rano.
en: The sea is bigger than the river.

aru: lipa i ra ne na.
en: This document is mine / related to me.

aru: poto ra to lipa.
en: The image is on the sheet / in the book.

aru: suko ra ama ke wai.
tulu ra rupi.
na ra mela ke tulu.
ya Aru ra waro lisu.
na ra no rima ke naru maru.
en: I do not want big rules.

aru: mene ra pawa waro nela.
naru mini ra nela.
waro lisu ra pawa.
mene mun ra nema ke ya Aru.
kale ne ya Aru ra waki.
mene mun ra waro we naru sama.
en: People speak with the same rules.

aru: senu ra lano ke naru ko mene sini.
en: A teacher gives rules to a new person.

aru: mene sini ra sawi ke se?
en: What does the new person ask?

aru: mene lasa ra pane ke mene sini.
en: The older person answers the new person.

aru: kale ra maku ke sipu mun.
sipu ne kale ra lisu.
mene mun ra sena ke waro.
waro i ra ruma nela.
na ra raku ke sipu mini.
ta ra lowa ke sipu ne na?
u ra raku ke lani ne u.
sipu i ra ama ke waro lisu.
sipu ena ra lasa pasu sipu i.
en: That text is older than this text.

aru: na ra kame ke sipu i.
na ra niso ke lani se.
o raku ke mona ne ta.
o lowa ke naru sini.
sipu ra pawa wena ke mene.
tulu ra sena ke waro.
tulu i ra lowa ke sipu.
tulu i ra pane ke i le, waro ra lisu.
en: This tool answers that speech is simple.

aru: na ra maku ke tulu ne sela waro.
en: I make a tool for seeing speech.

aru: tulu ne waro ra pawa.
mene ra pawa lowa ke waro we tulu.
en: A person can read speech with a tool.

aru: tulu ra no sona ke lani se.
en: The tool does not know some name.

aru: etu tulu ra no sona le, mene ra pane.
en: If the tool does not know, a person answers.

aru: tulu ra maku ke pane riki.
en: The tool makes a fast answer.

aru: naru lisu ra wena ke tulu.
temu luma nu le, kale ra puka ke sipu.
en: Today the community opens a text.

aru: mene mun ra lowa ke sipu puka.
en: Many people read the open text.

aru: mene se ra tanu so tera para.
en: Someone comes from a far place.

aru: mene se ra lora ke waro ne ya Aru.
en: Someone hears the speech of Aru.

aru: ta o sawi ke pane.
na ra lano ke pane ko ta.
en: I give the answer to you.

aru: etu pane ra nela le, na ra toma ke i.
en: If the answer is good, I keep it.

aru: etu pane ra keto le, na ra sena ke i.
en: If the answer is bad, I test it.

aru: waro ne kale ra ruma maru.
en: The speech of the community becomes important.

aru: ya Aru ra rima ke waro lisu.
mene un ra naka so malo.
en: One person goes from the house.

aru: u ra kipa ke tulu rupi.
u ra soku ke melo.
melo ra to rano.
en: The friend is by the river.

aru: melo ra lano ke wai ko u.
en: The friend gives water to them.

aru: u ra mela ke tulu.
u an melo ra tanu ko malo.
en: They and the friend come to the house.

aru: temu luma pini le, u ra miso.
en: At the end of the day, they sleep.

aru: sipu i ra mini, tune sipu i ra nela.
en: This text is small, but this text is good.

aru: mene ra lowa ke sipu i, sia mene ra waro.
en: A person reads this text, therefore the person speaks.
